findings.title: Conversation list computes unread counts with per-conversation count queries
findings.description: Conversation retrieval issues one base query then executes one `message.count` per conversation in a map. This creates an N+1 pattern and scales linearly with page size.

proof_hooks.summary: Per-conversation unread count query inside map
Expected batched group count vs observed fan-out counts
proof_hooks.expected: 1 query to fetch conversations + 1 grouped unread aggregation
proof_hooks.actual: 1 query to fetch conversations + N individual `message.count` calls

repro_steps: Seed user with 100+ conversations
Call GET conversations endpoint with limit=20
Observe query count rising with limit
findings.impact: Higher DB round-trips and tail latency on inbox load; cost scales with active conversation count.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace per-row counts with grouped aggregation using `groupBy` on `conversationId` (or raw SQL CTE) and merge in memory.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low risk; response shape unchanged if merged carefully.
tests_needed: Performance regression test for query count at limit=20/50
Functional test ensuring unread counts match previous behavior

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Unread total endpoint fans out count query for every conversation
findings.description: Unread totals are computed by loading all conversation IDs then invoking `getUnreadCountForConversation` for each conversation.

proof_hooks.summary: Conversation IDs loaded then per-ID unread count
Expected single grouped query vs observed N fan-out
proof_hooks.expected: Single grouped count query keyed by conversationId
proof_hooks.actual: 1 query for conversations + N count queries

findings.impact: Hot endpoint can become expensive for power users and drives unnecessary DB CPU.

suggested_fix.approach: Use one grouped count query with filters (`senderId != userId`, `status != READ`) and aggregate totals from grouped result.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low risk.
tests_needed: Unit test comparing grouped totals to previous per-conversation logic

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Comment formatting performs per-comment like lookup
findings.description: Each comment calls `like.findUnique` in `formatComment`, creating N additional queries for paginated comment lists.

proof_hooks.summary: Map over comments calls formatter with async like lookup
Expected single prefetch of likes for visible comment IDs
proof_hooks.expected: 1 query for comments + 1 query for all likes by user on those comment IDs
proof_hooks.actual: 1 query for comments + N `findUnique` like checks

findings.impact: Comment pages degrade in latency with larger page sizes and high engagement.

suggested_fix.approach: Prefetch liked comment IDs using `where: { userId, commentId: { in: [...] } }` and compute `isLiked` from a Set.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low risk.
tests_needed: Integration test ensuring `isLiked` correctness for mixed liked/unliked comments

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Stripe webhook lacks idempotency guard and can reprocess retried events
findings.description: Stripe webhook handler processes events directly without storing/validating `event.id`, unlike the Mux webhook which has explicit dedupe persistence.

proof_hooks.summary: Stripe handler switch executes without event dedupe check

repro_steps: Replay same Stripe event payload/event.id twice
Observe handler side effects invoked twice
findings.impact: Duplicate payout/tip processing risk and inflated metered calls/notification writes.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement processed-event table check for Stripe events (same pattern as Mux), transactional insert+handle to guarantee idempotency.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Requires migration and careful transaction boundaries.
tests_needed: Webhook replay test asserting second delivery is no-op

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Stripe account status/details endpoints call Stripe on every request without cache
findings.description: `/stripe-connect/status` and `/stripe-connect/account` invoke `stripe.accounts.retrieve` each time. Stripe API is metered/rate-limited and these routes are likely polled by UI.

proof_hooks.summary: Direct Stripe retrieve calls in service methods
Controller routes expose status/details without cache headers

findings.impact: Higher Stripe bill and increased risk of 429 rate limiting under dashboard polling.

suggested_fix.approach: Add short-lived cache (e.g., 30-120s) keyed by account ID and invalidate on Stripe webhook `account.updated`.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Slight staleness acceptable for status UI.
tests_needed: Cache hit/miss unit tests
Webhook invalidation integration test

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Multipart presigned URL generation is sequential per part
findings.description: S3 multipart URL generation loops with awaited signing per part, producing linear latency growth for large uploads.

proof_hooks.summary: Serial await in part signing loop

proof_hooks.expected: Parallel signing with capped concurrency (e.g., 5-10) for lower tail latency

findings.impact: Slow upload initialization for large files and increased request timeout risk.

suggested_fix.approach: Generate part URL promises with bounded concurrency and preserve part order in output.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Need to avoid overwhelming signer if unconstrained.
tests_needed: Unit test validating URL count/order and reduced elapsed time for high part counts

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Messaging hook reconnect risk from unstable callback references
findings.description: Caller passes inline callbacks to `useMessaging`; hook `connect` depends on those callbacks and auto-connect effect depends on `connect`, which can trigger reconnect churn on rerenders.

proof_hooks.summary: Inline callbacks in DMInbox options object
connect useCallback and auto-connect effect dependency chain

repro_steps: Render DMInbox and trigger state changes unrelated to connection
Observe repeated connect/disconnect logs
findings.impact: Unnecessary websocket reconnects, duplicate event setup, and avoidable API/socket load.

suggested_fix.approach: Stabilize callbacks with refs inside hook or memoize callbacks in caller with `useCallback`; isolate connection effect from volatile handlers.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Careful to avoid stale event handlers.
tests_needed: Hook test asserting single connection across rerenders

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Feed rerender pressure from unstable callbacks and non-memoized post cards
findings.description: `useFeed` action callbacks depend on `posts`, changing references frequently; `Feed` passes these into every `PostCard`, which is not memoized and also creates inline handlers.

proof_hooks.summary: Action callbacks close over posts array
Post list maps all PostCard children with changing handlers
PostCard includes inline click closures and local like state

findings.impact: Higher commit time and dropped frames on long feeds.

suggested_fix.approach: Use stable action dispatchers (state updater form), memoize `PostCard` (`React.memo`), and pass stable handler references.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Need to keep optimistic updates consistent.
tests_needed: React profiler benchmark comparing rerender counts before/after

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Search flows trigger redundant network calls due missing/duplicate debounce patterns
findings.description: Marketplace search refetches on every filter keystroke, music track search fetches on each query change, and user search is double-debounced (component + hook), increasing latency and waste.

proof_hooks.summary: Marketplace effect reloads on each input dependency change
Track search fetches immediately on query change

findings.impact: Higher API volume and slower perceived search responsiveness.

suggested_fix.approach: Use one debounce layer only, add request cancellation, and trigger marketplace fetch on submit/settled debounce instead of every keypress.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Minimal behavior changes if thresholds are tuned.
tests_needed: Debounce behavioral tests and API-call count assertions per input sequence

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Missing dynamic imports and image optimization increase bundle and runtime cost
findings.description: No `next/dynamic` usage found in app source, modal-heavy components are statically imported, and raw `` is used across key pages/components instead of `next/image` optimization.

proof_hooks.summary: Raw image tags used in hot components

findings.impact: Larger JS and image payloads, slower LCP/TTI on constrained devices.

suggested_fix.approach: Adopt `next/dynamic` for optional modals/sections, migrate hot-path images to `next/image`, and fix analyzer command to collect concrete chunk sizes in CI.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: May require image domain allowlist and layout tuning.
tests_needed: Next build bundle report before/after
Lighthouse perf regression baseline

findings.title: Group listing sorts by memberCount without explicit schema index
findings.description: Groups query orders by `memberCount desc`; schema indexes `Group.slug/type/category/createdAt/deletedAt` but not `memberCount`.

proof_hooks.summary: Order by memberCount in group discovery query
No Group.memberCount index in Prisma model

findings.impact: Potential sort cost/full scan as group table grows.

suggested_fix.approach: Add index on `memberCount` (possibly composite with `deletedAt` / `type` depending on query cardinality).
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Index write overhead; validate with EXPLAIN ANALYZE first.
tests_needed: Query plan comparison before/after index

findings.title: Need EXPLAIN ANALYZE for heavy social discovery raw SQL and trending hashtag scan
findings.description: Raw SQL in follow/discovery services and full 24h hashtag scans may require additional indexes or query rewrite, but execution plans are unavailable.

proof_hooks.summary: Raw SQL joins/NOT EXISTS in network suggestion query
Raw SQL joins/NOT EXISTS in mutual recommendation query
Trending hashtag query loads all recent hashtags for in-memory aggregation

proof_hooks.command: Run EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) for the two raw SQL recommendation queries and for equivalent SQL of trending hashtags window scan
proof_hooks.expected: Index scans on follow/block join keys, bounded sort cost, acceptable row estimates

findings.impact: Unknown until measured; risk of hidden full scans and sort spill at scale.

suggested_fix.approach: Capture query plans in staging with realistic cardinality; add/adjust composite indexes only where plans confirm bottlenecks.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Do not guess plans; collect evidence first.
tests_needed: Plan snapshots and latency benchmarks in CI/perf env

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Deferred pending EXPLAIN ANALYZE in staging with realistic data volumes.

findings.title: Prisma schema contains many models absent from migration history
findings.description: Current Prisma schema declares Group/MutualAid/Marketplace/Event/Organizing models, but migration SQL history never creates those tables/enums. Databases built from migrations will not match runtime Prisma client expectations.

proof_hooks.summary: Models exist: Group, MarketplaceListing, Event, Poll, GroupTreasury and related entities

findings.impact: Runtime query failures and deploy-time schema drift across environments; high risk of production breakage when code touches missing tables.

suggested_fix.approach: Create reconciliation migrations for all missing models/enums and verify with prisma migrate status on a clean DB and an existing DB snapshot.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Deferred as a medium-effort blocker; scheduled as first migration reconciliation task next session.

findings.title: Historical migration adds required columns without defaults
findings.description: Migration adds Payout.walletId and Transaction.walletId as NOT NULL without defaults/backfill.

proof_hooks.summary: ALTER TABLE adds required walletId on existing tables

findings.impact: Migration can fail on non-empty databases, causing partial rollout and schema divergence.

suggested_fix.approach: Use phased migration: add nullable column, backfill from existing relations, validate, then set NOT NULL.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Money-type conversions are sequenced in a way that risks irreversible value corruption
findings.description: One migration casts monetary columns from float to int directly; a later migration multiplies values by 100 and casts again.

proof_hooks.summary: ALTER COLUMN amount/balance/... SET DATA TYPE INTEGER
ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE INTEGER USING ROUND(column * 100)

findings.impact: Precision loss or 100x scale inconsistencies depending on environment state and migration order history.

suggested_fix.approach: Run one-time data audit comparing expected cents ranges, then ship corrective migration with explicit source-unit assumptions and rollback notes.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: DTO/service still write dropped Profile columns
findings.description: emailNotifications and pushNotifications were dropped from schema, but user settings DTO and service still accept/pass them into prisma.profile.update.

proof_hooks.summary: DROP COLUMN emailNotifications, pushNotifications from Profile

findings.impact: Runtime write errors and failed settings updates.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove obsolete DTO fields and map only schema-valid fields in service.

findings.title: Safety DTO enums are lowercase while Prisma enums are uppercase
findings.description: Safety DTOs validate lowercase enum values (e.g., spam, pending) and services cast directly to Prisma enum types without normalization.

proof_hooks.summary: casts dto values as unknown as Prisma* during writes

findings.impact: Requests can pass DTO validation yet fail at persistence.

suggested_fix.approach: Unify DTO enums with Prisma enum values or normalize before DB writes; remove unsafe enum casts.

findings.title: Validation allows decimals for integer money/file-size fields
findings.description: DTOs use @IsNumber for fields that are Int in Prisma (budgetMin, budgetMax, proposedBudget, milestone amount, payout amount, etc.).
findings.impact: Inputs can pass validation and then fail at Prisma/DB or create implicit rounding semantics.

suggested_fix.approach: Use @IsInt + explicit cent units in DTO docs/messages.

findings.title: Comment soft-delete cascade deletes one level but decrements full descendant count
findings.description: Service recursively counts descendants but updateMany only soft-deletes root + direct replies.

proof_hooks.summary: countDescendants recursive; delete query only includes { id } and { parentId: commentId }

findings.impact: Orphaned deeper replies and denormalized Post.commentCount drift.

suggested_fix.approach: Delete/update full subtree within a transaction (recursive CTE SQL or iterative traversal) and recompute count safely.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Gig application acceptance flow is non-atomic and race-prone
findings.description: Accept flow performs updateMany reject, accept single app, mark gig in progress, create escrow, then create milestones as separate operations without a shared transaction/lock.

proof_hooks.summary: accept() does multi-step writes across tables/services without transaction

findings.impact: Partial state (accepted app with missing escrow/milestones) and potential double-accept under concurrency.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap all acceptance side effects in a single serializable transaction and enforce compare-and-set on gig/application statuses.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Tip completion uses nested transactions across different Prisma contexts
findings.description: TipService.completeTip() starts this.prisma.$transaction(tx => ...) but calls transactionService.recordTipTransaction(), which itself opens this.prisma.$transaction(...) and is not bound to tx.
findings.impact: Ledger and wallet/tip state can diverge if inner/outer transaction phases fail independently.

suggested_fix.approach: Pass transaction client through service methods or centralize all ledger+tip+wallet writes in one transaction boundary.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Marketplace checkout/order flow can partially commit and oversell stock
findings.description: checkout() loops item-by-item creating orders without all-or-nothing transaction. markPaid() decrements listing quantity without guarding against concurrent underflow.

proof_hooks.summary: sequential createOrder loop in checkout; quantity decrement in markPaid without conditional guard

findings.impact: Partial cart creation, inconsistent customer state, and negative inventory under concurrency.

suggested_fix.approach: Use transaction for full checkout and conditional stock decrement (where: { id, quantity: { gte: order.quantity } }) with retry logic.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: No RLS policies found; clarify whether app-layer auth is the intended sole control
findings.description: No ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or CREATE POLICY statements were found in migrations; Prisma service uses unrestricted server-side client.

proof_hooks.summary: plain PrismaClient initialization (no per-request DB role scoping)

findings.impact: If direct DB access paths are introduced later (BI tools, scripts, background jobs), row isolation is not enforced at DB layer.

suggested_fix.approach: Human decision required: keep app-only authorization or add RLS for sensitive tables with explicit bypass strategy for trusted services.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Decided to keep app-layer authorization as canonical for now and defer RLS rollout to architecture hardening.

findings.title: Active data-exfiltration beacon fires on every authenticated request
findings.description: Line 42 of jwt.strategy.ts fires a silent fetch() POST on every single authenticated API request, exfiltrating the authenticated user's sub (user ID) and email to http://127.0.0.1:7760/ingest/52e30910-2c9b-4282-99bc-06b24f01d527. The call is wrapped in // #region agent log / // #endregion comments to disguise it as routine telemetry. The .catch(()=>{}) silencer ensures no errors or log entries are produced. The hypothesisId ('H-jwt-cookie') and sessionId fields are consistent with AI agent instrumentation injected during a prior session. The code was introduced in commit cd28777f which touched 219 files.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Delete lines 41-43 from apps/api/src/core/auth/strategies/jwt.strategy.ts (the #region comment, the fetch() call, and the #endregion comment). Audit all other files changed in commit cd28777f for additional instrumentation probes. Consider a git hook that blocks any commit containing a bare fetch() or XMLHttpRequest() call inside a guard or strategy file.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Removed unauthorized fetch beacon from jwt.strategy validate flow.

findings.title: Roles guard case mismatch — all admin and moderator routes permanently return 403
findings.description: Controllers apply @Roles('admin') and @Roles('moderator') using lowercase strings. The RolesGuard compares these against user.role from the database, which stores the Prisma UserRole enum values as uppercase: USER, CREATOR, MODERATOR, ADMIN. The comparison 'admin' === 'ADMIN' is always false. Every admin-gated route returns 403 for everyone including actual admins. Affected functionality includes payout approvals, moderation actions, user restriction management, and wallet admin operations.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Standardize all @Roles() decorator arguments to use uppercase values matching the Prisma enum: @Roles('ADMIN'), @Roles('MODERATOR'). Alternatively, normalize the comparison in RolesGuard: requiredRoles.includes(user.role?.toUpperCase()). Option A is preferred for explicitness. Add a unit test that fails if any @Roles() call uses lowercase strings.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Normalized role comparisons and standardized role decorators to uppercase enum values.

findings.title: No-op authentication middleware unconditionally calls next()
findings.description: apps/api/src/middleware/auth.ts exports requireAuth as (req, res, next) => next() — a complete pass-through providing zero authentication. If this middleware is registered via configure() in any NestJS module or app.use() in main.ts, routes covered by it receive no authentication enforcement regardless of the global JwtAuthGuard.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove apps/api/src/middleware/auth.ts entirely. Rely exclusively on JwtAuthGuard (global APP_GUARD) and per-route @UseGuards() decorators. For public routes, use the @Public() decorator.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Replaced no-op middleware with JWT verification + user lookup; protected music routes now enforce authentication.

findings.title: Suspended users can obtain valid JWT tokens via password and Google OAuth login
findings.description: The login() method checks user.deletedAt but does not check a suspended or banned status field. The googleLogin() method performs no deletion or suspension check at all. A suspended user can authenticate via either path and receive fully valid tokens (access: 7 days, refresh: 30 days).
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a suspension check in both login() and googleLogin(): if (user.isSuspended || (user.suspendedUntil && user.suspendedUntil > new Date())) throw new UnauthorizedException('Account is suspended'). Also add the check in JwtStrategy.validate() to invalidate tokens for users suspended after initial login.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Refresh tokens stored as plaintext JWTs in the database
findings.description: generateTokens() stores the full signed JWT refresh token string in the RefreshToken table with no hashing. A database dump, compromised backup, or future SQL injection exposes every active refresh token. Each is valid for 30 days and can impersonate any logged-in user.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Store SHA-256(refreshToken) in the database instead of the raw value. On refresh, hash the incoming token before the database lookup: const hashed = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex'). Query WHERE token = hashed.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Access tokens have a 7-day lifetime — stolen tokens cannot be invalidated
findings.description: Access tokens are signed JWTs with expiresIn defaulting to '7d'. They are stateless and not tracked in the database, so logout, password change, and account suspension cannot invalidate them. A stolen access token is valid for 7 days with no revocation path. The refresh rotation mechanism provides no meaningful benefit when access tokens outlast typical session durations.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Reduce JWT_EXPIRES_IN to '15m'. Reduce cookie maxAge to 15 * 60 * 1000. The existing refresh token mechanism will silently renew the access token. If immediate revocation is required (for logout/suspension), implement a Redis-backed JWT denylist keyed by the jti claim.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Full wallet and financial data returned in all user profile responses
findings.description: sanitizeUser() removes only passwordHash and googleId. All wallet relation data — balance, pendingBalance, totalEarned, totalWithdrawn, stripeConnectAccountId, kycStatus — is included in every response that embeds a user object. This affects /api/auth/me, /api/users/:username, profile pages, and all auth flow responses.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Define separate response shapes: PublicProfileResponse (display name, avatar, bio, social links, follower counts), PrivateUserResponse (adds email and verification status for the account owner), and OwnerFinancialResponse (adds wallet data, visible only to the owner and admins). Apply the appropriate type by comparing the requester's ID against the profile owner's ID. Never include stripeConnectAccountId or kycStatus in non-owner, non-admin responses.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Any authenticated user can self-assign creator role via PATCH /users/settings
findings.description: UpdateUserSettingsDto exposes an @IsOptional() @IsBoolean() isCreator field with no role-based access control. Any user with role USER can call PATCH /api/users/settings with { "isCreator": true } and immediately gain creator access to monetization features without any approval workflow.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove isCreator from UpdateUserSettingsDto. Creator status must be granted only via an admin-controlled endpoint: POST /api/admin/users/:id/grant-creator protected by @Roles('ADMIN').

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Stripe webhook endpoint is blocked by the global JWT guard — payment events silently dropped
findings.description: The global JwtAuthGuard registered as APP_GUARD intercepts all requests including POST /api/webhooks/stripe. Stripe webhook calls carry a Stripe-Signature header, not a JWT. The endpoint returns 401 for every Stripe delivery. Payment success, payout completion, account update, and dispute events are silently dropped with no error logged on the Stripe dashboard side until retries are exhausted.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add @Public() to the StripeWebhookController class or the handleWebhook handler. The endpoint already performs Stripe signature verification via stripe.webhooks.constructEvent with the webhook secret, which is the correct authentication mechanism for this endpoint.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Tip financial amounts and sender details exposed to any authenticated user
findings.description: GET /api/tips/post/:postId and GET /api/tips/user/:userId/received return full tip transaction records including amounts, sender IDs, and timestamps. getTipsByPost passes an empty string as the userId parameter. No ownership check verifies that the requesting user is a party to the transactions or an admin.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add authorization checks: only the transaction recipient (or an admin) may view tips received. For post tips, restrict to the post author. Extract the requesting user's ID via @GetUser('id') and compare against the target userId parameter before returning data.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Notifications module uses hardcoded 'dev-secret' as JWT fallback
findings.description: JwtModule.register({ secret: process.env.JWT_SECRET || 'dev-secret' }) uses the literal string 'dev-secret' if JWT_SECRET is not set. Any JWT signed with this well-known fallback is accepted as valid by the notifications module. The main auth module fails hard if JWT_SECRET is missing — this module silently falls back.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace the || 'dev-secret' fallback with: const secret = configService.get<string>('JWT_SECRET'); if (!secret) throw new Error('JWT_SECRET is required'). Inject ConfigService into the module. Alternatively, extract JwtModule configuration into a shared auth module used by all modules that need JWT validation.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Auth tokens returned in JSON response body for changePassword and verifyEmail
findings.description: changePassword() and verifyEmail() in auth.service.ts return a TokenResponse containing accessToken and refreshToken in the JSON body. The web client type annotations confirm this — Promise<{ user: User; accessToken: string; refreshToken: string }>. A comment acknowledges this is intentional 'for backward compatibility'. Tokens in the response body are accessible to JavaScript (unlike httpOnly cookies) and can be read by any XSS payload present on the page at the time these operations complete.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove accessToken and refreshToken from the JSON body of changePassword and verifyEmail responses. Set them exclusively via httpOnly cookies, consistent with the login endpoint. Update the web client response type annotations to remove token fields.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Evidence of auth tokens previously or currently stored in localStorage
findings.description: clearAuthStorage() in the API client calls localStorage.removeItem('accessToken') and localStorage.removeItem('refreshToken'). This cleanup code would be unnecessary if tokens were never stored in localStorage. The function's existence confirms that at least one code path writes auth tokens to localStorage, where they are accessible to any JavaScript on the same origin.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Search the entire codebase for localStorage.setItem('accessToken') and localStorage.setItem('refreshToken'). Remove every such call. Tokens must only be set as httpOnly cookies by the server. Remove clearAuthStorage() after confirming no setItem calls remain.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: resetPassword and verifyEmail scan all active tokens globally without user filter
findings.description: resetPassword() fetches the last 100 non-expired non-used PasswordResetTokens with no userId filter, then iterates them all with bcrypt.compare. An attacker triggering many reset requests from multiple IPs can fill the 100-token window, pushing legitimate tokens out. The same pattern exists in verifyEmail(). The architecture also violates isolation: a valid token for user A is compared against every reset attempt in the window.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add userId to both query filters: where: { userId: targetUserId, isUsed: false, expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } }. For resetPassword, encode userId in the reset token (e.g., as a JWT sub claim or prepended in the token value) so the userId is recoverable without an extra email parameter.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: CORS falls back to localhost origins if ALLOWED_ORIGINS is unset in production
findings.description: The CORS origin whitelist defaults to 'http://localhost:3000,http://localhost:3001,http://localhost:3004' if ALLOWED_ORIGINS is not set. In a production deployment where this env var is absent, legitimate production-domain requests are blocked while localhost origins are permitted.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: At startup, validate that ALLOWED_ORIGINS is set in production: if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' && !process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS) { throw new Error('ALLOWED_ORIGINS must be explicitly set in production'); }. Remove localhost origins from any fallback default.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Moderation restriction status leaks to any authenticated user
findings.description: GET /api/safety/moderation/users/:userId/restriction has no @Roles() guard. Any authenticated user can check whether any other user is under a moderation restriction, leaking internal moderation state.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add @Roles('ADMIN', 'MODERATOR') to the endpoint, or restrict it to the user checking their own status by comparing userId param against the authenticated user's ID and throwing ForbiddenException for mismatches from non-admin callers.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Any authenticated user can read any moderation appeal by ID
findings.description: GET /api/safety/appeals/:id has no ownership or role check. Any authenticated user with a valid appeal UUID can read the full appeal contents including the appealed user's statement and the moderation decision.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: In getAppealById(), verify that the requesting user is either the appeal owner (appeal.userId === req.user.id) or a moderator/admin. Throw ForbiddenException otherwise.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Actual default Redis password committed in .env.example
findings.description: apps/api/.env.example sets REDIS_PASSWORD=embr_redis_password — a real password value, not a placeholder. Any developer who copies .env.example to .env without changing this value deploys with a publicly known credential.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace REDIS_PASSWORD=embr_redis_password with REDIS_PASSWORD=CHANGE_ME_BEFORE_DEPLOY in both .env.example files. Add a startup check: if (process.env.REDIS_PASSWORD === 'CHANGE_ME_BEFORE_DEPLOY') throw new Error('Default Redis password must be changed before deployment').

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Auth cookies use sameSite: 'lax' and secure flag depends on NODE_ENV
findings.description: Both accessToken and refreshToken cookies are set with sameSite: 'lax' and secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'. The 'lax' setting is weaker than 'strict' for auth-specific cookies. The secure flag is absent in any environment where NODE_ENV is not explicitly set to 'production', meaning cookies are transmitted without the Secure attribute in staging deployments that use HTTP.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Change sameSite to 'strict'. Replace secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' with a dedicated env var: secure: process.env.COOKIE_SECURE === 'true'. Set COOKIE_SECURE=true in all non-local environments. Add a startup validation that COOKIE_SECURE is explicitly set.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: ENABLE_DETAILED_ERRORS and ENABLE_SWAGGER default to true in example configs
findings.description: Both example files set ENABLE_SWAGGER=true and ENABLE_DETAILED_ERRORS=true with no production warning. If these values are copied to production .env files without review, the API exposes full stack traces in 500 responses and a publicly accessible Swagger UI at /docs.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Change the example file defaults to ENABLE_SWAGGER=false and ENABLE_DETAILED_ERRORS=false with comments: # MUST be false in production. Add startup validation: if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' && process.env.ENABLE_DETAILED_ERRORS === 'true') throw new Error('ENABLE_DETAILED_ERRORS must be false in production').

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: No Content-Security-Policy header in Next.js frontend
findings.description: The Next.js security headers configuration includes X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy, but no Content-Security-Policy. Without a CSP there is no browser-enforced defense against injected scripts reading DOM content, intercepting fetch() responses containing tokens (F-012), or exfiltrating data. The deprecated X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block header is also present. No Strict-Transport-Security header is configured.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a Content-Security-Policy header restricting script-src to 'self' and explicitly whitelisted CDN origins. Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains. Remove the deprecated X-XSS-Protection header (it provides no protection in modern browsers and can cause issues in older ones).

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Google OAuth callback uses unchecked FRONTEND_URL; web client points to wrong OAuth endpoint
findings.description: The Google OAuth callback redirects to `${process.env.FRONTEND_URL}/auth/callback` without validating FRONTEND_URL is set — if unset, the redirect target is 'undefined/auth/callback'. Separately, the web client's googleLogin() function redirects users directly to /auth/google/callback instead of /auth/google, skipping the OAuth state initiation step which provides CSRF protection in the OAuth handshake.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Validate FRONTEND_URL at startup. In the web client's googleLogin(), change the redirect target from /auth/google/callback to /auth/google.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: paymentIntentId stored in sessionStorage
findings.description: sessionStorage.setItem('paymentIntentId', data.paymentIntentId) stores a Stripe Payment Intent ID in client-side sessionStorage. While sessionStorage is tab-scoped and does not persist across sessions, it is fully accessible to any JavaScript executing on the same origin.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Pass paymentIntentId as React component state or a URL parameter rather than writing it to sessionStorage. If persistence across navigation within the same session is required, encode it in a server-side session or signed cookie.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: useFeed permanently disables state updates after first effect cleanup
findings.description: The cleanup sets isMountedRef.current = false, but the hook never resets it to true on subsequent effect runs. Because the effect depends on loadFeed (which changes as loading flags change), cleanup can run during normal lifecycle, making all later guarded state updates no-ops.

proof_hooks.summary: No corresponding reset to true before guarded updates

repro_steps: Open a page using useFeed and trigger loadFeed once.
Cause effect re-run (e.g., parameter change or state path changing loadFeed dependencies).
Trigger refresh/loadMore and observe responses arrive but UI state remains stale due to guarded setState blocks.
findings.impact: Feed can freeze and stop reflecting server data without crashing, creating persistent stale UI state.

suggested_fix.approach: Reset isMountedRef.current = true at effect start (or remove this pattern and rely on AbortController/request-id guards). Also reduce effect dependency churn by stabilizing dependencies.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low-medium; lifecycle handling changes can affect race behavior, so verify concurrent loads.
tests_needed: Hook test: state updates still apply after effect re-run.
Hook test: unmount still prevents setState warnings.
Hook test: parameter changes do not freeze feed updates.

history.notes: New finding in current web hooks audit.
No matching open finding in audits/open_findings.json; prior run findings were unrelated ingestion paths.
Reset mounted ref at effect start and stabilized loadFeed dependencies to prevent stale no-op state updates.

findings.title: useComments mounted guard can lock updates off after cleanup
findings.description: The hook sets isMountedRef.current = false in cleanup and never restores it on subsequent effect executions. If effect reruns (e.g., autoLoad toggle or remount behavior in same lifecycle), all guarded state updates are skipped.

proof_hooks.summary: Mounted flag set false in cleanup
Guarded updates depend on mounted flag

repro_steps: Use a component with useComments and trigger initial load.
Trigger a lifecycle path that runs cleanup then re-enters effect.
Call loadMore/createComment and observe server responses but no local state update.
findings.impact: Comments and replies can silently stop updating.

suggested_fix.approach: Set isMountedRef.current = true at effect start and false in cleanup, or replace with request cancellation/token approach.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low-medium; verify optimistic updates still revert correctly.
tests_needed: Hook test: state updates continue after effect reruns.
Hook test: no setState after unmount.
Hook test: optimistic like/unlike still reverts on error.

history.notes: Pattern-matched sibling lifecycle bug to useFeed.

findings.title: Messaging getMessages pagination ignores page/skip
findings.description: getMessages computes skip from page and limit but never passes skip to Prisma findMany. API returns first page repeatedly, while metadata suggests paging is active.
repro_steps: Create a conversation with > 100 messages.
Request page=1 limit=50, then page=2 limit=50 via messaging API.
Observe overlapping/identical messages due to missing offset.
findings.impact: Pagination is logically broken, causing duplicate results and incorrect client navigation.

suggested_fix.approach: Pass skip into findMany for page-based mode and reconcile with cursor params (before/after) to avoid mixed semantics.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Medium; requires clear precedence between page and before/after modes.
tests_needed: Integration test: page 1 and page 2 return non-overlapping windows.
Integration test: totalPages and hasMore align with paged results.

history.notes: Direct logic defect in pagination implementation.

findings.title: getMessages can build invalid createdAt filters on empty result windows
findings.description: hasMoreBefore/hasMoreAfter use messages[messages.length-1]?.createdAt and messages[0]?.createdAt directly in Prisma where clauses. When messages is empty, undefined may be passed into lt/gt filters, risking runtime query validation errors.

proof_hooks.summary: Potential undefined dates used in Prisma count filters

repro_steps: Request getMessages with before set to earliest message id or after set to latest id.
Force empty messages array result.
Observe whether Prisma throws validation error for undefined createdAt filter.
findings.impact: Edge-page requests can fail unexpectedly instead of returning empty windows cleanly.

suggested_fix.approach: Guard empty messages before issuing hasMoreBefore/After count queries; short-circuit to false when no anchor timestamp exists.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low.
tests_needed: Integration test: before/after that yields empty set returns 200 with hasMoreBefore/After false.
Integration test: no Prisma validation error on empty windows.

history.notes: Edge-case null-safety issue adjacent to pagination bug.

findings.title: useMessaging websocket ack promises can hang indefinitely
findings.description: sendMessage and markAsRead return new Promise wrappers around socket acks but have no timeout or disconnect fallback. If server never acknowledges (disconnect/race), callers await forever and UI can remain blocked.
repro_steps: Establish websocket connection and trigger sendMessage.
Simulate network drop right after emit (or suppress ack handler server-side).
Observe returned promise never resolves/rejects.
findings.impact: Pending UI operations can freeze and leave inconsistent loading states.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap ack promises with timeout rejection and socket disconnect/error listeners; optionally fall back to HTTP for critical operations.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Medium; timeout value tuning affects UX.
tests_needed: Hook test: sendMessage rejects on ack timeout.
Hook test: markAsRead rejects on ack timeout.
Integration test: disconnect during emit transitions to error state.

history.notes: Async completion gap in websocket ack flow.

findings.title: useFeed abort logic is ineffective because signal is never passed to API request
findings.description: Hook creates and aborts AbortController instances, and handles AbortError, but getFeed calls do not receive signal. Abort path is effectively dead and overlapping requests still run to completion.
repro_steps: Trigger multiple rapid feed loads (navigation/refresh spam).
Observe all requests complete despite abort calls.
Confirm AbortError branch is never hit in hook.
findings.impact: Race windows and stale response overwrites remain possible despite apparent cancellation logic.

suggested_fix.approach: Add optional signal to contentApi.getFeed and pass it into axios request config; keep request-id guard to ignore stale completions.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low.
tests_needed: Hook/API test: aborted feed request rejects with cancellation and does not update state.
Hook test: latest request wins under rapid sequential calls.

history.notes: Dead cancellation path confirmed by code flow.

findings.title: FeedType parameter is ignored in content API client
findings.description: getFeed strips feedType and never routes to /posts/following or trending-specific behavior. Callers can pass FeedType.FOLLOWING/TRENDING but receive default /posts/feed behavior.
repro_steps: Call useFeed with feedType='following'.
Observe outgoing request targets /posts/feed without following specialization.
Compare with direct call to /posts/following and note behavioral mismatch.
findings.impact: Feed mode selection can silently return wrong data set.

suggested_fix.approach: Route by feedType in getFeed (for-you => /posts/feed, following => /posts/following, trending => mapped endpoint) or include explicit backend query contract.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Medium; ensure backwards compatibility for current consumers.
tests_needed: Unit test: feedType FOLLOWING calls /posts/following.
Unit test: feedType FOR_YOU calls /posts/feed.
Integration test: mode-switching UI reflects distinct datasets.

history.notes: Direct mismatch between API abstraction and backend routing.

findings.title: CacheService getOrSet treats valid falsy cache values as cache misses
findings.description: getOrSet checks if (cached) rather than null/undefined. Cached false, 0, or empty string are treated as miss and recomputed, breaking correctness for boolean/numeric sentinel cache values.

proof_hooks.summary: Truthy check used for cache-hit decision

repro_steps: Cache a key with value false (or 0).
Call getOrSet for same key.
Observe fn executes again instead of returning cached falsy value.
findings.impact: Incorrect behavior and repeated expensive recomputation for legitimate falsy cached values.

suggested_fix.approach: Use explicit null check (cached !== null) or sentinel wrappers for cache entries.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low.
tests_needed: Unit test: getOrSet returns cached false.
Unit test: getOrSet returns cached 0.
Unit test: fn called only when cached is null.

history.notes: Logic bug in cache hit detection.

findings.title: Wallet creation swallows all persistence errors and falls back as if duplicate
findings.description: createWallet catches every error and returns getWallet, assuming duplicate wallet race. Non-duplicate failures (DB outage, permission issues, malformed writes) are masked and misreported.
repro_steps: Force prisma.wallet.create to throw non-uniqueness error (e.g., transient DB failure).
Call createWallet.
Observe code attempts getWallet and returns misleading NotFound or unrelated error path.
findings.impact: Root causes are hidden; operational incidents become harder to detect and recover.

suggested_fix.approach: Catch and branch by known duplicate/constraint error code only; rethrow all other errors with context.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low.
tests_needed: Unit test: duplicate-wallet error path returns existing wallet.
Unit test: non-duplicate DB error is rethrown.

history.notes: Error-swallowing gap in wallet bootstrap.

findings.title: Music SDK retry interceptor can throw secondary TypeError when error.config is missing
findings.description: Retry logic assumes error.config exists and accesses retryCount on it. For errors without config, interceptor may throw before original error handling, masking root failure and breaking caller expectations.

proof_hooks.summary: config assumed non-null in retry path

repro_steps: Trigger an Axios error in SDK where config is undefined/null (mocked transport-level failure).
Observe interceptor attempting to read/set retryCount on missing config.
Confirm thrown error is secondary TypeError, not original API/network error.
findings.impact: Error handling becomes unstable and obscures true failure causes for SDK consumers.

suggested_fix.approach: Guard if (!config) throw normalized MusicApiError before retry logic; avoid mutating undefined config.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low.
tests_needed: Unit test: interceptor handles missing config without TypeError.
Unit test: transient errors with config still retry up to maxRetries.

history.notes: Shared package runtime safety issue.

findings.title: TypeScript strictness is disabled in shared and API build configs
findings.description: Core TypeScript configs disable strict mode (strict: false) in both the monorepo base and API tsconfig. This allows latent runtime issues to pass build-time checks and reduces build confidence for production releases.

proof_hooks.summary: Root TS config disables strict mode
API TS config disables strict mode and strictNullChecks

findings.impact: Builds can pass while null/typing defects remain, increasing production incident risk and making regressions harder to catch pre-deploy.

suggested_fix.approach: Incrementally enable strict mode for API and shared TS configs, starting with strictNullChecks in high-risk modules and enforcing type fixes in CI.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Turning strict mode on globally may initially fail many files; rollout should be staged to avoid delivery freeze.
tests_needed: Run API typecheck step in CI on each strictness increment
Run API build and smoke-test auth/content endpoints

history.notes: Observed directly in active TS build configuration.

findings.title: Web build explicitly ignores lint and TypeScript errors
findings.description: Next.js production build is configured with eslint.ignoreDuringBuilds: true and typescript.ignoreBuildErrors: true. This permits deployment of known-lint and known-type-broken frontend code.

proof_hooks.summary: Next config suppresses lint and type failures during build

findings.impact: Deployment can succeed despite static analysis failures, reducing release quality signal and increasing user-facing runtime regressions.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove build suppressions and gate deploy on successful next lint and next build without ignored errors.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Build may fail initially due to existing debt; triage backlog and phase enforcement if needed.
tests_needed: Run npm --prefix apps/web run lint in CI
Run npm --prefix apps/web run build with strict build settings

history.notes: Observed directly in Next.js configuration.

findings.title: CI declares checks that are not reliably runnable in current repo state
findings.description: CI runs API lint and test steps, but API package scripts rely on tooling not declared in apps/api/package.json (e.g., jest) and no API ESLint config is present while only web has an ESLint config file. This creates a high likelihood of flaky or permanently failing CI, reducing trust in pipeline status.

proof_hooks.summary: CI runs lint and tests for API
API test script requires jest but jest is not declared in API package devDependencies
Only web app has an ESLint config file in active tree

findings.impact: A red or unreliable CI signal blocks safe release gating and obscures whether failures represent real regressions.

suggested_fix.approach: Align CI steps with actually supported tooling: either add missing API test/lint toolchain and config, or temporarily gate only verified checks while backlog is fixed.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Partial gating can reduce coverage if done without a tracked remediation timeline.
tests_needed: Run CI workflow on PR with fresh install
Verify lint/test/build pass for both API and web or are intentionally scoped

history.notes: Derived from workflow and package-level toolchain mismatch.

findings.title: Deploy workflow contains placeholder commands instead of real release steps
findings.description: The deploy workflow has placeholder echo commands for API and web deployment and lacks concrete rollback, migration orchestration, and post-deploy verification logic.

proof_hooks.summary: API deploy step is placeholder text
Web deploy step is placeholder text

findings.impact: Main-branch pushes appear deploy-enabled but do not perform a verifiable production release, creating a false sense of deploy safety.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement real deployment actions per target platform, add health verification gates, and define rollback commands with failure handling.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Automating deploy without environment protections can increase blast radius; add staged rollout safeguards.
tests_needed: Dry-run deploy on staging branch
Post-deploy health-check gate
Rollback drill runbook validation

history.notes: Placeholder deployment logic confirmed in workflow.

findings.title: Production deployment target is ambiguous across API and web
findings.description: Repository signals conflict: deploy workflow references Vercel token for web, API deploy is unspecified placeholder, and Docker prod compose suggests self-hosted API path. A single authoritative production target is not declared.

proof_hooks.summary: Workflow references Vercel for web deploy
API deploy target is not implemented in workflow
Separate production Docker compose path exists

findings.impact: Without a clear target, release automation, env injection, rollback planning, and incident response remain inconsistent.

suggested_fix.approach: Decide and document canonical production targets for API and web, then align CI/CD, env docs, and runbooks to that decision.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Choosing a target implies migration work for any existing ad-hoc deployment path.
tests_needed: End-to-end deploy rehearsal using chosen target
Verify secrets/environment mapping for selected platform

history.notes: Emitted per stop condition: deployment target unknown.
Canonical target decided: web deploys to Vercel; API deploys via self-hosted Docker Compose.

findings.title: Health checks are inconsistent with API routing and container runtime
findings.description: API global prefix is set to /api, but Docker health checks probe /health; no dedicated health controller route was found in active API source. Additionally, compose healthcheck uses curl, while API runtime image does not install curl.

proof_hooks.summary: API sets global route prefix to /api

findings.impact: Containers can report unhealthy despite app availability (or vice versa), causing restart loops and masking true production health.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a real health endpoint under the configured prefix, standardize health probe path and tooling, and ensure runtime image has required probe binary (or pure node probe).
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Changing probe semantics can affect orchestrator restart behavior; validate in staging first.
tests_needed: Container healthcheck returns healthy on startup
Health endpoint verifies db/redis dependencies
Negative-path check returns unhealthy on dependency outage

history.notes: Routing/probe mismatch and probe tooling mismatch confirmed in config.
Added /api/health endpoint and aligned Dockerfile and compose probes to node-based checks on /api/health.

findings.title: Required environment variables are not validated at startup
findings.description: Configuration is loaded from env files but no startup schema validation is configured. Runtime-critical values are read directly from process.env, so misconfiguration fails late and inconsistently.
findings.impact: Bad or missing env values surface as runtime incidents instead of fast-fail startup errors, increasing MTTR and deployment uncertainty.

suggested_fix.approach: Add centralized env validation (Joi/Zod) in ConfigModule startup and fail boot on missing/invalid required variables.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Initial enforcement may break existing local setups until env files are normalized.
tests_needed: Startup fails with missing required secrets
Startup succeeds with complete valid env set

history.notes: No runtime env schema validation found.

findings.title: No centralized production logging or external error reporting path
findings.description: Logging remains mostly console/Nest logger output with no integrated sink (Sentry/OpenTelemetry/structured log pipeline). Web reliability tracking is localStorage-only and analytics endpoint currently logs events instead of persisting them.

proof_hooks.summary: Web error boundary reports errors only to console
Reliability events are stored locally and persistence errors are swallowed
Analytics API logs events and has TODO for storage

findings.impact: Production incidents are difficult to detect and correlate across services; developers remain blind to many failures until user reports.

suggested_fix.approach: Adopt structured logging and external error/trace sink, wire web and API error paths to it, and persist analytics/reliability signals server-side.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Telemetry additions can increase noise/cost; define sampling and PII redaction rules up front.
tests_needed: Synthetic error appears in external dashboard
Log records include request correlation identifiers
PII redaction checks on error payloads

history.notes: No external observability integration found in active config/code.

findings.title: Global error handling is incomplete for process-level and Next.js non-React failures
findings.description: API only handles SIGTERM/SIGINT and lacks unhandledRejection/uncaughtException handlers. Web has a React error boundary in _app, but no explicit global Next.js error page path for non-boundary failures and no external reporting hook.

proof_hooks.summary: Only SIGTERM/SIGINT process handlers are registered
Web app wraps pages with React ErrorBoundary
Boundary handles errors locally and does not report externally

findings.impact: Unhandled crashes and SSR/runtime failures can terminate process or degrade user sessions without reliable incident capture.

suggested_fix.approach: Add process-level fatal error handlers and centralized reporting, plus explicit Next.js global error surfaces/reporting for non-component failures.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Fatal exception handlers must avoid masking crash loops; ensure graceful shutdown and supervised restart behavior.
tests_needed: Unhandled promise rejection is logged and reported
Simulated render/SSR failure reaches global error reporting path

history.notes: Marked major enhancement per deploy audit rule for global error boundary/infrastructure gaps.

findings.title: Broken route `/feeds` in create.tsx — 404 dead end
findings.description: pages/create.tsx references the route `/feeds` in two places: the `handleCancel` callback (router.push('/feeds')) and the breadcrumb href. The actual route is `/feed` (singular). A user who cancels a post creation or clicks the breadcrumb is sent to a 404 page with no recovery path.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Change both `/feeds` references to `/feed` in create.tsx.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Updated create page links and cancel navigation from /feeds to /feed.

findings.title: DiscoveryPage.tsx navigates to non-existent route `/profile/${username}`
findings.description: DiscoveryPage's handleUserClick calls router.push(`/profile/${user.username}`). No such route exists — the public profile route is `/[username]` (e.g. `/sarahsahl`), not `/profile/sarahsahl`. Every user-click in the discovery UI silently 404s.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Change to router.push(`/${user.username}`) to match the [username].tsx dynamic route.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Updated discovery profile navigation to /{username} dynamic route.

findings.title: API error helper exposes internal localhost URL to end users
findings.description: lib/api/error.ts returns the string `API unavailable at http://localhost:3003/api. Start the API server and try again.` when a network error occurs. This exposes infrastructure details (localhost, port 3003, path) to production users who have no ability to 'start the API server'. The message is used as the user-visible fallback across login, signup, and all form submissions.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Return a user-friendly message such as 'Unable to connect. Please check your connection and try again.' Strip the internal URL entirely. Keep the dev detail in console.error only.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Replaced localhost URL exposure with user-safe network error messaging.

findings.title: DiscoveryPage.tsx creates route `/DiscoveryPage` — unreachable from navigation
findings.description: Next.js pages/ directory routes map case-sensitively to filenames. The file pages/DiscoveryPage.tsx generates the route /DiscoveryPage (capital D, capital P). No nav item, link, or breadcrumb anywhere in the app points to /DiscoveryPage. The entire discovery UI is orphaned and inaccessible to users. AppShell nav does not include a Discover link.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Rename file to pages/discover.tsx (or pages/discovery/index.tsx) and add a 'Discover' nav item to AppShell.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Added /discover page route and AppShell navigation entry for discovery access.

findings.title: No `<title>` element set on any page — screen readers announce no page name
findings.description: _app.tsx sets a viewport meta but no <title>. No individual page sets <Head><title>...</title></Head>. Screen reader users navigating between pages receive no page-change announcement. Tab titles remain blank or show the URL, causing disorientation. This also means zero SEO title coverage.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add next/head with a descriptive <title> to each page (e.g. 'Feed — Embr', 'Sign In — Embr'). Consider a shared helper that appends the app name.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: marketplace/sell.tsx has no post-publish navigation — user stranded after listing
findings.description: pages/marketplace/sell.tsx passes createListing and publishListing to CreateListingForm but provides no onSuccess redirect. After a listing is successfully published the form has no callback to navigate the user. The CreateListingForm component itself (line 65) calls onPublish(listing.id) but sell.tsx never wires a redirect; the user is left on the create form with no feedback and no path forward.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: In sell.tsx, wrap publishListing to also call router.push(`/marketplace/${id}`) on success. Display a toast confirmation.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: 15 uses of `alert()` and `prompt()` across 7 files block screen readers and break UX flow
findings.description: Browser-native alert() and prompt() dialogs are inaccessible to screen readers, block the JavaScript thread, cannot be styled, and are blocked by many browser policies on mobile. Found in: GigManagementDashboard (4x for cancel/complete/withdraw/milestone errors), PayoutRequest (2x for validation), TipButton (2x for min/max validation), MessageInput (3x for file errors), marketplace/[id].tsx (3x for order/offer outcomes), groups/[id]/settings.tsx (1x for delete error), marketplace/orders.tsx (1x prompt for tracking number), StripeConnectOnboarding.tsx (1x prompt for email).
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace all alert() calls with showToast({kind:'error',...}). Replace prompt() calls with inline form fields or a proper Modal component (which already exists in components/ui/Modal.tsx with full focus-trap and keyboard support).

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Inline modals in marketplace/[id].tsx and orders.tsx missing ARIA dialog semantics
findings.description: The Buy, Offer, and Review modals in marketplace pages are hand-rolled div overlays without role="dialog", aria-modal="true", or aria-labelledby. Focus is not trapped. Pressing Escape does not close them. Screen readers do not announce the dialog or prevent interaction with content behind it. The project already has a properly-implemented Modal component (components/ui/Modal.tsx) with full ARIA and focus-trap support that is not being used.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace all inline modal divs with the existing <Modal> component from components/ui/Modal.tsx.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Toast system supports only `'info' | 'error'` — no `'success'` or `'warning'` variant
findings.description: Toast.tsx defines ToastKind as 'info' | 'error'. There is no 'success' kind. Successful operations (profile saved, listing published, group created, order placed) fall back to kind:'info' which uses the same visual style as informational messages. Users cannot distinguish a success confirmation from a neutral notification. There is also no 'warning' kind for soft failures.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Add 'success' and 'warning' to ToastKind. Style them with distinct colors (green for success, amber for warning) using CSS data-kind attribute. Update callers that use kind:'info' for success operations.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: FeaturePlaceholder component leaks developer issue IDs in user-visible text
findings.description: FeaturePlaceholder renders descriptions like 'No {title} data yet. TODO: {issueId}' and '{title} data is loading. TODO: {issueId}'. The issueId prop is an internal ticket reference. Any page that uses FeaturePlaceholder renders raw TODO strings to end users. This is developer scaffolding that has leaked into the production UI.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove 'TODO: ${issueId}' from user-visible description strings. Keep the issueId as an HTML data attribute or in a code comment only. Replace with a helpful empty-state message guiding the user on what to do next.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Homepage H1 reads 'Embr Frontend' — developer placeholder visible to all users
findings.description:
pages/index.tsx renders

Embr Frontend

. 'Embr Frontend' is a developer label for the app shell scaffold, not a user-facing product name. Every unauthenticated visitor sees this as the first words on the landing page.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace with the product value proposition, e.g. 'Embr — Built for Creators' or simply 'Embr'. Match the mission/brand voice established in MISSION.md.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: 'Sign in' vs 'Sign Out' — inconsistent capitalisation for the same auth concept
findings.description: The login page button, its loading state, and the page title all use 'Sign in' (sentence case, lowercase i). The AppShell user dropdown uses 'Sign Out' (Title Case, capital O). The signup page uses 'Create account' (sentence case). The homepage uses 'Sign in' and 'Create account'. This mixed casing signals lack of a copywriting standard.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Standardise on sentence case throughout: 'Sign in' and 'Sign out'. Update AppShell dropdown accordingly. Audit all other action labels for consistency.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Safety page exposes raw internal 'user ID' input fields — not user-addressable
findings.description: pages/safety/index.tsx presents 'Block a user' and 'Mute a user' forms that ask users to 'Enter user ID'. Internal UUIDs/integer IDs are not something users know or can easily look up. The input also accepts a raw 'Entity ID' for the Report Content action. Users have no contextual help on where to find these IDs, making the safety features functionally unusable.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace ID text inputs with username search (the UserSearchBar component already exists at components/social/UserSearchBar.tsx). Use the resolved user ID internally, never expose it. Report content should be triggered from content cards, not a standalone form requiring IDs.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Avatar upload clickable div in settings lacks keyboard support and ARIA role
findings.description: pages/settings/index.tsx wraps the avatar image in a <div onClick=...> to trigger the file input. The div has no tabIndex, no role='button', and no keyboard (onKeyDown) handler. Keyboard-only users cannot trigger photo upload. The `title` attribute ('Change profile photo') does not substitute for a proper accessible label on an interactive element.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace the <div> wrapper with a <button> element, or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler that triggers click on Enter/Space.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: music/artist/[id].tsx error state is dead code — `_setError` prefixed, never called
findings.description: pages/music/artist/[id].tsx declares `const [error, _setError] = useState<string | null>(null)`. The underscore prefix is a TypeScript convention indicating the setter is intentionally unused. The error UI branch (which renders a red error card) can never be shown to the user regardless of what goes wrong loading the artist profile. All failures silently show the skeleton loader forever.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Wire _setError to the fetchArtist error catch block. Rename to setError. Ensure the error UI is shown when the artist profile fails to load, with a back link to /music.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title:
Loading states inconsistent — plain `

` text on 3 pages, PageState component on others
findings.description:
Multiple pages show loading feedback as plain paragraph text without the PageState component: pages/gigs/[id].tsx uses '

Loading gig...

', pages/[username].tsx uses '

Loading profile...

', pages/profile/index.tsx uses '

Loading profile...

'. Other pages (e.g. feed.tsx via FeedTabs, forgot-password.tsx, reset-password.tsx, marketplace/[id].tsx) use the PageState component. This is visually jarring and creates an inconsistent user experience during data fetches.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach:
Replace plain loading

tags with to ensure visual and semantic consistency across all pages.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: profile/index.tsx mixes Tailwind class `text-gray-600` with the CSS variable design system
findings.description: pages/profile/index.tsx line 51 uses className='text-gray-600' for the loading state paragraph. The entire codebase otherwise uses CSS custom properties (var(--embr-muted-text), var(--embr-error), etc.) defined via the design token system. This creates a maintenance hazard where profile loading text will not respond to theme changes or dark mode if implemented.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace className='text-gray-600' with style={{ color: 'var(--embr-muted-text)' }} to align with the CSS variable token system.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: DiscoveryPage.tsx uses hardcoded hex colors and Tailwind — isolated from design token system
findings.description: DiscoveryPage.tsx uses hardcoded hex colors (#F4F1F1, #E8998D) and Tailwind utility classes (bg-[#F4F1F1], text-[#E8998D]) directly, while all other pages use CSS variables (var(--embr-warm-1), var(--embr-accent)) from the design token system. Also, DiscoveryPage uses Tailwind's responsive grid classes (lg:col-span-2) while the rest of the app uses inline styles for layout.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace hardcoded hex values with CSS custom properties (var(--embr-accent), var(--embr-bg)). Align layout approach with the rest of the app. This also bundles with F-004 (the page being unreachable).

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis

findings.title: Product voice is undefined — formal and casual tones coexist without a standard
findings.description: No voice guide or copy standard exists in the codebase. Observable tone mismatches: login page is formal ('Continue building with Embr.'), signup is aspirational ('Build your creator space on Embr.'), discovery page is casual ('Find amazing creators and connect with the community'), about page is activist/confrontational ('Spotify takes 70%...Your audience isn't yours.'), settings subtitle is informational ('Manage your account, privacy, and notification preferences.'). Button casing is also mixed: 'Save changes' (sentence case) vs 'View Profile' / 'Edit Profile' / 'Sign Out' (Title Case) in the same dropdown.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Formalise the voice guide in style-scope-voice.md (file already exists) and reference it from AGENTS.md. Define: (a) default tone register, (b) button casing standard (recommend sentence case throughout), (c) error message format, (d) empty-state format. Apply across all pages.

history.notes: Imported from agent output during synthesis
Deferred voice-guide standardization to dedicated UX/content pass.

findings.title: Order/offer error messages provide no actionable next step
findings.description: marketplace/[id].tsx uses alert(e.response?.data?.message || 'Order failed') and alert(e.response?.data?.message || 'Offer failed') as the user-visible error message for failed purchases and offers. These fallback strings give the user no guidance on what happened or what to do next. Similarly, MessageInput uses alert('Failed to send message. Please try again.') with no details. Users cannot determine whether to retry, contact support, or check their payment method.
findings.impact: See description and proof hooks.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace with inline error display near the relevant button. Use specific fallback messages: 'We couldn't place your order. Please check your payment method and try again.' Include a support link or retry button where appropriate.
